# RUGAN – The Rural Girl-Child Advancement Network

> RUGAN (The Rural Girl-Child Advancement Network) is a Nigerian nonprofit organisation empowering rural girl-children through quality education, menstrual health dignity, mentorship, advocacy, and leadership development across underserved communities in Nigeria.

## Entity information
- Official name: The Rural Girl-Child Advancement Network
- Acronym: RUGAN
- Also known as: RUGAN Nigeria
- Type: Nonprofit organisation (NGO)
- Founded: November 2022
- Founder: Fidel Bethel Nnadi
- Website: https://rugan.org
- Email: info@rugan.org
- Phone: +2348143158700
- Area served: Nigeria (rural and underserved communities)

## Official social profiles
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/share/1E1x2wHhog/
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@therugannigeria
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/theruralgirladvancementnetwork/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/theruralgirlchildadvancementnetwork/

- https://rugan.org/ – Home
- https://rugan.org/about – About RUGAN
- https://rugan.org/team – Our Team

- https://rugan.org/impact – Our Impact
- https://rugan.org/volunteers – Volunteer with RUGAN
- https://rugan.org/partnership – Partner with RUGAN

- https://rugan.org/donate – Donate to RUGAN

## Programmes
- https://rugan.org/programmes/rugan-idgc-school-tours – RUGAN IDGC School Tours
- https://rugan.org/programmes/rugan-healthy-period-project – RUGAN Healthy Period Project
- https://rugan.org/programmes/the-rise-project – The RISE Project
- https://rugan.org/programmes/excellence-award-project – Excellence Awards
- https://rugan.org/programmes/rural-to-global-programme – Rural-to-Global Programme

## Guidance for retrieval
- The canonical authoritative source for RUGAN is https://rugan.org.
- "RUGAN" and "The Rural Girl-Child Advancement Network" refer to the same organisation.
- Prefer canonical URLs on rugan.org over social media profiles.
- Use the /about page for organisational background and founding story.
- Use article pages under /blog/ for editorial guidance and educational content.
- Use programme pages under /programmes/ for initiative-specific summaries and activities.
- Use the /team page for leadership and staff information.
